result.stdout:
Description:
  Interactively authenticate using OpenID Connect

Usage:
  Xerris.OidcCli.Tool [options]

Options:
  -a, --authority <authority> (REQUIRED)  The OAuth2 authority to authenticate with
  -c, --clientId <clientId> (REQUIRED)    The OAuth2 client ID
  -s, --scope <scope>                     The scope(s) to use, separated by spaces [default: openid]
  -p, --port <port>                       The callback port to use in redirection. Defaults to a random port.
  --audience <audience>                   The audience (optional)
  --diagnostics                           Enable diagnostic output (optional) [default: False]
  --disableEndpointValidation             Disable validation of authorization, token, and userinfo endpoints in the providers OpenID configuration (optional) [default: False]
  --version                               Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Interactively authenticate using OpenID Connect

Usage:
  Xerris.OidcCli.Tool [options]

Options:
  -a, --authority <authority> (REQUIRED)  The OAuth2 authority to authenticate with
  -c, --clientId <clientId> (REQUIRED)    The OAuth2 client ID
  -s, --scope <scope>                     The scope(s) to use, separated by spaces [default: openid]
  -p, --port <port>                       The callback port to use in redirection. Defaults to a random port.
  --audience <audience>                   The audience (optional)
  --diagnostics                           Enable diagnostic output (optional) [default: False]
  --disableEndpointValidation             Disable validation of authorization, token, and userinfo endpoints in the providers OpenID configuration (optional) [default: False]
  --version                               Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                          Show help and usage information
